raw_consumer_statement: I have a credit card with Citibank. It's an XXXX XXXX XXXX card. I had fraud charges from a vendor I did not recognize and contacted Citi bank in XXXX XXXX. They cancelled my card, said they resolved the fraud, did issue credits back to my account. However, after getting my new card in the mail, I again received a fraudulent charge from the same vendor in late XXXX XXXX on the new card. I have been on the phone with Citibank for a collective XXXX hours over the last few days. All they will do is simply cancel and issue a new card as they did in XXXX. They will not explain why, after saying the fraud had been resolved in XXXX, the same vendor was able to charge my card again. I simply asked how issuing a second new card is going to make this problem go away. No explanation is given. They just continue to send me to different people. It's obvious too the calls are going overseas. I don't think I have a physical Citi bank branch in my area otherwise I'd go in and ask someone what to do. This whole experience is disappointing and makes me what to avoid any business with Citibank.
